context: CORE is a dealflow infrastructure and screening platform for VC funds in the DACH region. It enables funds to receive standardized startup submissions via CoreLink, run automated plausibility checks, apply a configurable priority scoring engine (Custom Index), and push qualified deals to Affinity CRM. The target users are investment professionals – analysts, associates, partners – who are time-poor, data-driven, and skeptical of noise. The UI must reflect that.

steps.instructions: Read the file CLAUDE.md in the root of this repository. Understand the project goals, tech stack (Vanilla JS / ES modules, bundled via esbuild into bundle.js), file structure, and all conventions defined there. Do not proceed without reading it fully.

Read the file at claude/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md. This is your design bible for this task. Internalize every guideline before touching any code. Pay special attention to the anti-patterns section – these are exactly what CORE currently suffers from and must be eliminated.

Read the current implementation: index.html, main.css, and all JS modules (main.js, state.js, renderers.js, filters.js, custom-index.js, signal-index.js, activity.js, data.js, utils.js, config.js). Identify: (a) all UI components and views currently rendered, (b) the color palette and typography in use, (c) layout patterns and spacing conventions, (d) any existing animations or interactions. Make a written list of every element that feels generic, low-effort, or like AI slop. Be specific – name the component and describe the problem.

Before writing a single line of code, commit to a specific design concept built for CORE. The aesthetic must feel like serious, opinionated B2B SaaS infrastructure – not a startup template, not a Notion clone, not a shadcn dashboard. Think: Bloomberg Terminal meets modern editorial design. Consider: a dense, data-forward layout with surgical whitespace; institutional typography that signals credibility; a near-black base with one cold or warm sharp accent (not blue); tabular numerals for all score and data columns. Write a short design brief as a comment block in the CSS before implementing. Include: chosen font pairing, color tokens, spacing scale, and the one visual idea that makes this design unforgettable.

Rewrite the UI across all files according to the defined direction. Requirements: (1) Typography – load a distinctive font pair via Google Fonts or Bunny Fonts (@import in CSS); no Inter, no system-ui, no Roboto. Apply tabular-nums to all numeric data. (2) Color – define every value as a CSS custom property (--color-*) in :root. No hardcoded hex values outside the token definitions. Dark mode is primary. (3) Spacing – use a strict 8pt scale; all spacing values are multiples of 8px. (4) Motion – one orchestrated page-load animation with staggered reveals using animation-delay; hover states on all interactive elements; all transitions under 200ms; CSS-only, no JS animation for simple effects. (5) Backgrounds – no flat solid colors; use gradient meshes, subtle noise textures, or layered transparencies matching the aesthetic. (6) Components to restyle: deal table (rows, headers, badges, score columns), Custom Index sliders and weight controls, filter bar, sidebar or navigation, buttons and inputs, modal or detail panels, empty states, and any activity or log views. Every component must belong to the same design system – no orphaned styles. (7) The result must be visually distinctive and feel purpose-built for a VC dealflow tool.

After the main implementation: (1) Consistency pass – every component must feel like it belongs to the same system. Eliminate any leftover generic patterns. (2) Legibility check – all text readable at intended sizes; sufficient contrast on dark backgrounds. (3) Interactive states – hover, focus, and active states defined for every clickable or focusable element. (4) Viewport check – layout must not break at 1280px, 1440px, and 1920px. (5) Clean up – remove commented-out boilerplate, unused CSS classes, and placeholder styles. (6) Add a design token reference comment block at the top of main.css documenting colors, fonts, and spacing scale for future sessions.

do_not: Use Inter, Roboto, Arial, system-ui, or any generic system font as the primary typeface
Use purple gradients on white or light backgrounds
Use unstyled or minimally-styled shadcn/tailwind default patterns
Add heavy dependencies – work within the existing Vanilla JS / CSS stack; a font import or a small utility is acceptable
Leave any existing component partially or fully unstyled in the old aesthetic
Use rounded pill-style buttons or card shadows that look like a SaaS template
Ask clarifying questions before starting – CLAUDE.md and the skill file contain all context needed
must: Write a design brief comment block in main.css before writing any component styles
Define all colors, fonts, and spacing as CSS custom properties
Apply tabular-nums to every column displaying scores, percentages, or numeric data
Deliver a complete, runnable prototype – partial redesigns are not acceptable
Ensure bundle.js is rebuilt via esbuild after any JS changes (see CLAUDE.md for the build command)
